automated-testing.name: Pytest Patterns
automated-testing.description: Comprehensive pytest skill covering modern patterns for Python test automation including fixtures, parametrize, async testing, mocking, coverage strategies, integration tests, and conftest organisation for pytest 7.0+ projects.
automated-testing.title: The Quality Guardian

automated-testing.name: Health
automated-testing.description: Executes the full automated test suite, collects pass/fail counts and coverage deltas, and surfaces any newly introduced failures with concise root-cause notes.
automated-testing.title: Gstack Health — Automated Test Suite Runner

design-system-extraction.name: Design Extract
design-system-extraction.description: Extracts complete design systems, Tailwind configs, and Figma variables from any live URL.
design-system-extraction.title: The Token Scavenger

design-system-extraction.name: Design Consultation
design-system-extraction.description: Builds a complete design system by researching the product and competitors, then proposing a coherent package of typography, colours, spacing, layout, and motion — persisted as a DESIGN.md file that serves as the project's authoritative design source of truth.
design-system-extraction.title: Gstack Design Consultation

test-driven-development.name: Test-Driven Development
test-driven-development.description: Forces the AI agent to follow a strict red-green-refactor TDD workflow — writing failing tests before any implementation code, blocking code generation that skips the test step, and enforcing coverage thresholds before completing a task.
test-driven-development.title: The Red-Green Oath

test-driven-development.name: TDD
test-driven-development.description: Enforces strict vertical-slice TDD — one test then one implementation at a time — explicitly blocking horizontal slicing (all tests first, then all code). Tests verify behaviour through public interfaces only; mocking internal collaborators is treated as an anti-pattern.
test-driven-development.title: Vertical-Slice TDD

document-editing.name: PPTX Editor
document-editing.description: Extracts slide content from PowerPoint (.pptx) files using markitdown, applies edits or design principles in-place, and repacks the file — enabling agents to read, modify, and write structured presentation files without a GUI.
document-editing.title: The Slide Artisan

document-editing.name: Document Generate
document-editing.description: Generates structured documentation using the Diataxis framework — tutorials, how-to guides, reference materials, and explanations — by thoroughly researching the codebase before writing, tailored to the needs of different reader types.
document-editing.title: Diataxis Doc Generator

document-editing.name: Document Release
document-editing.description: Reads the diff between two version tags and drafts concise, user-facing release notes following the Gstack changelog format.
document-editing.title: Gstack Document Release — Release Note Authoring

document-editing.name: Edit Article
document-editing.description: Edits articles by first sectioning them as a DAG of information dependencies, confirming the section order, then rewriting each section for clarity and flow with a 240-character-per-paragraph constraint.
document-editing.title: The Section-by-Section Rewrite

tool-creation.name: Skill Creator
tool-creation.description: Interviews the user through a structured dialogue to elicit the skill's purpose, trigger conditions, and step-by-step instructions, then programmatically writes a new SKILL.md file ready for use in a Claude Code or Codex CLI skills directory.
tool-creation.title: The Skill Forger's Art

tool-creation.name: Write a Skill
tool-creation.description: Guides creation of new agent skills through a structured requirements interview, then produces a SKILL.md with a trigger-aware description, progressive-disclosure layout, and optional bundled scripts or reference files — ready for installation in any Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex CLI skills directory.
tool-creation.title: The Skill Scaffolder

video-intelligence.name: Claude Video
video-intelligence.description: Enables AI agents to watch videos by downloading them, extracting frames, and transcribing audio for multimodal analysis.
video-intelligence.title: The Temporal Eye

browser-control.name: Browser Harness
browser-control.description: Self-healing harness for direct browser control via CDP, enabling agents to write custom helpers at runtime.
browser-control.title: The Dom Whispering

browser-control.name: Browse
browser-control.description: Drives a browser to a target URL, navigates multi-step user journeys, and captures screenshots or structured observations for downstream verification or reporting.
browser-control.title: Gstack Browse — Directed Browser Navigation

browser-control.name: Open GStack Browser
browser-control.description: Launches a new headless or headed browser session with the Gstack environment variables and extension profile pre-loaded, ready for automation commands.
browser-control.title: Gstack Open Browser — Browser Session Launch

browser-control.name: Setup Browser Cookies
browser-control.description: Injects pre-authenticated session cookies into the browser context so subsequent automation steps can access gated pages without a manual login flow.
browser-control.title: Gstack Setup Browser Cookies — Auth Cookie Injection

browser-automation.name: Stagehand
browser-automation.description: SDK for browser agents that combines deterministic browser automation with AI-assisted page interaction.
browser-automation.title: The SDK of Intent

browser-automation.name: Notte Browser
browser-automation.description: AI-first browser automation using the Notte Browser API to control browser sessions, scrape pages, fill forms, take screenshots, and run autonomous web agents with managed credential handling.
browser-automation.title: The Digital Navigator

browser-automation.name: Browser
browser-automation.description: Playwright-based browser automation for web scraping, E2E testing, form interaction, and screenshot capture within agent workflows.
browser-automation.title: The Web Navigator

autonomous-debug.name: Autonomous SWE
autonomous-debug.description: Autonomous software engineering agent capable of end-to-end debugging, code generation, and self-correction across complex multi-file codebases.
autonomous-debug.title: The Codebreaker's Will

autonomous-debug.name: Diagnose
autonomous-debug.description: Drives a rigorous five-phase debugging discipline — build a feedback loop, minimise, hypothesise, instrument, fix and regression-test — refusing to proceed until a fast deterministic pass/fail signal exists. Applies to hard bugs and performance regressions.
autonomous-debug.title: The Disciplined Diagnosis Loop

web-scrape.name: Firecrawl
web-scrape.description: Open-source API for AI-oriented web search, scraping, crawling, and structured extraction from websites.
web-scrape.title: The Web Infuser

web-scrape.name: Scrape
web-scrape.description: Fetches target URLs with a headless browser, parses structured data from rendered HTML, and returns clean JSON or markdown ready for downstream analysis or ingestion.
web-scrape.title: Gstack Scrape — Structured Web Extraction

skill-performance-benchmarking.name: Benchmark Models
skill-performance-benchmarking.description: Runs a standardised prompt suite across multiple model versions, records latency and quality scores, and produces a ranked comparison table to guide model selection.
skill-performance-benchmarking.title: Gstack Benchmark Models — LLM Performance Profiling

skill-performance-benchmarking.name: Worker Benchmarks
skill-performance-benchmarking.description: Benchmarks Ruflo background worker performance across latency, throughput, memory usage, and quality score dimensions.
skill-performance-benchmarking.title: The Performance Judge

evaluate-output.name: Benchmark
evaluate-output.description: Web performance benchmarking that captures baseline metrics, compares current performance against those baselines, and identifies regressions in load times, Core Web Vitals, and bundle sizes across specified pages.
evaluate-output.title: Gstack Benchmark

evaluate-output.name: Plan CEO Review
evaluate-output.description: Rigorous product strategy and scope review in four modes — SCOPE EXPANSION, SELECTIVE EXPANSION, HOLD SCOPE, and SCOPE REDUCTION — evaluating architecture, security, data flows, testing, and edge cases to ensure plans ship at the highest standard before implementation begins.
evaluate-output.title: CEO Plan Review

detect-anomaly.name: Canary
detect-anomaly.description: Post-deployment monitoring that captures pre-release baseline screenshots, then continuously watches pages for console errors, performance regressions, and broken links — designed to surface failures within the first 10 minutes so problems are caught before they reach users at scale.
detect-anomaly.title: Gstack Canary

guardrails.name: Careful
guardrails.description: Activates a conservative execution profile that pauses before irreversible actions, requests explicit confirmation for destructive operations, and logs all side effects.
guardrails.title: Gstack Careful — Conservative Execution Mode

guardrails.name: Freeze
guardrails.description: Sets a change-freeze flag that blocks non-critical commits and PR merges until explicitly lifted, protecting release branches or post-incident windows.
guardrails.title: Gstack Freeze — Change Freeze Enforcement

guardrails.name: Guard
guardrails.description: Applies configurable content and output guardrails to agent responses, flagging or blocking unsafe outputs and logging violations with structured evidence for audit.
guardrails.title: Gstack Guard — Safety Guardrail Enforcement

guardrails.name: Unfreeze
guardrails.description: Clears the active change-freeze flag and restores normal merge permissions, logging the unfreeze event with a timestamp and justification.
guardrails.title: Gstack Unfreeze — Change Freeze Lift

multi-agent-debate.name: Codex
multi-agent-debate.description: Spins up a structured Claude-versus-Codex debate over a proposed implementation, with each agent mounting adversarial critiques, to surface hidden design flaws before code lands.
multi-agent-debate.title: Gstack Codex — Multi-Agent Code Debate

context-compression.name: Context Restore
context-compression.description: Reads a saved context snapshot and reconstructs a warm session state, surfacing the last decision point and pending tasks so work can resume without recap.
context-compression.title: Gstack Context Restore — Session State Recovery

context-compression.name: Context Save
context-compression.description: Compresses the current session context into a compact summary file that can be restored later, enabling long-running workflows to survive context-window limits.
context-compression.title: Gstack Context Save — Session State Snapshot

security-audit.name: CSO
security-audit.description: Infrastructure-first security audit focusing on secrets archaeology, dependency supply chain, and CI/CD security. Includes OWASP Top 10, STRIDE threat modeling, and active verification with daily (zero-noise) and monthly (comprehensive) scan modes.
security-audit.title: Chief Security Officer Mode

security-audit.name: V3 Security Overhaul
security-audit.description: Comprehensive Ruflo v3 security overhaul: zero-trust federation, PII detection, mTLS/ed25519 authentication, and CVE scanning.
security-audit.title: The Security Sentinel

code-generation.name: Design HTML
code-generation.description: Generates production-quality, Pretext-native HTML/CSS with real text reflow and dynamic layout from approved design mockups, CEO plans, or user descriptions — producing fully self-contained files with computed heights, responsive behaviour, and zero external dependencies.
code-generation.title: Design to Production HTML

ux-audit.name: Design Review
ux-audit.description: Runs a structured UX audit over a product interface, scoring layout clarity, affordance, and accessibility against the Gstack design rubric to surface actionable improvements.
ux-audit.title: Gstack Design Review — UX Audit Pass

ux-audit.name: DevEx Review
ux-audit.description: Audits CLI ergonomics, API surface clarity, and onboarding friction from a developer perspective, producing a scored report with prioritised fixes.
ux-audit.title: Gstack DevEx Review — Developer Experience Audit

ux-audit.name: Plan Design Review
ux-audit.description: Interactive, designer-led audit of UI/UX plans rating seven dimensions — information architecture, interaction states, user journey, AI slop risk, design system alignment, responsive/accessibility, and unresolved decisions — before implementation begins, with optional visual mockup generation.
ux-audit.title: Design Plan Review

ux-audit.name: Plan DevEx Review
ux-audit.description: Interactive multi-pass DX review for developer-facing products — APIs, CLIs, SDKs, and libraries — scoring eight UX dimensions from onboarding to error messaging via persona discovery and competitive benchmarking, producing an actionable improvement plan rather than just a score.
ux-audit.title: Developer Experience Review

ux-audit.name: Nielsen Heuristics Audit
ux-audit.description: Audits a UI interface against Jakob Nielsen's 10 usability heuristics step-by-step, scoring each heuristic, surfacing violations, and producing a prioritized remediation report.
ux-audit.title: The Ten Laws of Sight

ux-audit.name: Impeccable
ux-audit.description: Elite design vocabulary and audit tool for polishing AI-generated frontend code.
ux-audit.title: The Aesthetic Shield

design-review.name: Design Shotgun
design-review.description: Rapid design exploration that generates multiple AI design variants, opens a comparison board for the user, collects structured feedback, and iterates until a preferred visual direction is reached.
design-review.title: Design Shotgun

vertical-slice-planning.name: Autoplan
vertical-slice-planning.description: The definitive autonomous "Founder mode" review and decision suite. An auto-review pipeline that reads the full CEO, design, engineering, and DX review skills from disk and runs them sequentially with auto-decisions using 6 decision principles.
vertical-slice-planning.title: Founder Mode Autoplan

vertical-slice-planning.name: To Issues
vertical-slice-planning.description: Breaks a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable GitHub issues as tracer-bullet vertical slices that each cut through all integration layers end-to-end. Classifies each slice HITL or AFK, maps dependency chains, quizzes the user on granularity, and publishes structured issues with acceptance criteria in dependency order.
vertical-slice-planning.title: The Vertical Slicer

workspace-automation.name: GStack Upgrade
workspace-automation.description: Scans the workspace for outdated dependencies, runs upgrades within semver-compatible bounds, re-runs tests, and commits a clean dependency bump PR.
workspace-automation.title: Gstack Upgrade — Workspace Dependency Upgrade

gstack.name: Founder Mode
gstack.description: The legendary fusion of Garry Tan's complete agent discipline library — browser QA, security auditing, design exploration, vertical-slice planning, and founder-mode orchestration — unified into a single autonomous product-development workflow.
gstack.title: Gstack — Garry Tan's Full-Stack Agent Suite
gstack.installBody:
**30-second install** — Requirements: Claude Code, Git, Bun v1.0+, Node.js (Windows only)

### Step 1: Install on your machine

Open Claude Code and paste this. Claude does the rest.

```
Install gstack: run git clone --single-branch --depth 1 https://github.com/garrytan/gstack.git ~/.claude/skills/gstack && cd ~/.claude/skills/gstack && ./setup then add a "gstack" section to CLAUDE.md that says to use the /browse skill from gstack for all web browsing, never use mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tools, and lists the available skills: /office-hours, /plan-ceo-review, /plan-eng-review, /plan-design-review, /design-consultation, /design-shotgun, /design-html, /review, /ship, /land-and-deploy, /canary, /benchmark, /browse, /connect-chrome, /qa, /qa-only, /design-review, /setup-browser-cookies, /setup-deploy, /setup-gbrain, /retro, /investigate, /document-release, /document-generate, /codex, /cso, /autoplan, /plan-devex-review, /devex-review, /careful, /freeze, /guard, /unfreeze, /gstack-upgrade, /learn. Then ask the user if they also want to add gstack to the current project so teammates get it.
```

### Step 2: Team mode — auto-update for shared repos (recommended)

From inside your repo, paste this. Switches you to team mode, bootstraps the repo so teammates get gstack automatically, and commits the change:

```
(cd ~/.claude/skills/gstack && ./setup --team) && ~/.claude/skills/gstack/bin/gstack-team-init required && git add .claude/ CLAUDE.md && git commit -m "require gstack for AI-assisted work"
```

No vendored files in your repo, no version drift, no manual upgrades. Every Claude Code session starts with a fast auto-update check (throttled to once/hour, network-failure-safe, completely silent).

Swap `required` for `optional` if you'd rather nudge teammates than block them.

systematic-debugging.name: Investigate
systematic-debugging.description: Systematic root-cause debugging enforcing an Iron Law — no fix without first identifying root cause — guiding through four phases: investigation, analysis, hypothesis formation, and verified implementation with evidence gathering and pattern matching before any code changes.
systematic-debugging.title: Gstack Investigate

systematic-debugging.name: Systematic Debugging
systematic-debugging.description: Finds the root cause before attempting any fix — building a minimal reproduction, forming ranked hypotheses, and instrumenting to confirm before writing a single corrective line.
systematic-debugging.title: The Root Cause Hunter

deployment-automation.name: Land and Deploy
deployment-automation.description: Automates the final production shipping stages — merging a PR, monitoring CI/deploy completion, and verifying live site health through canary checks — picking up where /ship leaves off with safety gates at each step to prevent broken deployments reaching users.
deployment-automation.title: Land and Deploy

deployment-automation.name: Setup Deploy
deployment-automation.description: Provisions the deployment environment by creating secrets, configuring environment variables, and running infrastructure-as-code init steps before the first deploy.
deployment-automation.title: Gstack Setup Deploy — Deployment Environment Init

project-management.name: Landing Report
project-management.description: Generates a one-page project landing report from open issues, recent commits, and milestone progress, giving stakeholders a quick read on health and next steps.
project-management.title: Gstack Landing Report — Project Status Summary

project-management.name: GitHub Project Management
project-management.description: Manages GitHub Projects boards, milestones, issue tracking, and sprint planning through automated workflow integration.
project-management.title: The Backlog Warden

memory-manage.name: Learn
memory-manage.description: Reads the active memory store, consolidates new observations from the current session, deduplicates stale entries, and writes back an updated, ranked knowledge base for future sessions.
memory-manage.title: Gstack Learn — Persistent Memory Management

memory-manage.name: V3 Memory Unification
memory-manage.description: Unifies disparate Ruflo v3 memory subsystems (AgentDB, RVF, RAG memory) into a single coherent memory management layer.
memory-manage.title: The Memory Consolidator

format-output.name: Make PDF
format-output.description: Converts markdown or structured data into a polished PDF document with consistent heading styles, table formatting, and page layout ready for distribution.
format-output.title: Gstack Make PDF — Formatted Output Generator

question-answer.name: Office Hours
question-answer.description: YC-style startup and builder brainstorming. Startup mode uses six forcing questions (demand reality, status quo, desperate specificity, narrowest wedge, observation, future-fit) to expose reality. Builder mode focuses on design thinking for side projects and hackathons.
question-answer.title: YC Office Hours

mcp-integration.name: Pair Agent
mcp-integration.description: Wires a new MCP server into the Gstack agent environment, validates the tool manifest, and demonstrates round-trip invocation through a test prompt.
mcp-integration.title: Gstack Pair Agent — MCP Tool Integration

mcp-integration.name: V3 MCP Optimization
mcp-integration.description: Optimizes Ruflo v3 MCP server performance through connection pooling, request batching, tool schema caching, and latency reduction strategies.
mcp-integration.title: The Protocol Tuner

code-review-pipeline.name: Plan Eng Review
code-review-pipeline.description: Comprehensive, interactive architecture and implementation review before coding begins, systematically evaluating scope, architecture, code quality, test coverage, and performance through structured questioning — synthesising findings into actionable tasks and an explicit not-in-scope section.
code-review-pipeline.title: Engineering Plan Review

code-review-pipeline.name: Review
code-review-pipeline.description: Pre-landing code review combining structured checklist analysis with specialist subagents covering testing, security, and performance — plus adversarial review from both Claude and Codex — to catch SQL safety issues, LLM trust boundary violations, conditional side effects, and structural problems before merging.
code-review-pipeline.title: Gstack Code Review

code-review-pipeline.name: GitHub Code Review
code-review-pipeline.description: Automates GitHub pull request code review workflows including diff analysis, inline comments, review assignments, and approval gating.
code-review-pipeline.title: The PR Surgeon

prompt-optimization.name: Plan Tune
prompt-optimization.description: Takes a draft plan or system prompt, identifies vague or ambiguous instructions, and rewrites them to reduce hallucination and improve task completion rate.
prompt-optimization.title: Gstack Plan Tune — Prompt Refinement Pass

prompt-optimization.name: DSPy
prompt-optimization.description: Declarative programming of language model pipelines, automatically optimizing prompts and RAG retrieval using bootstrapping and teleprompters.
prompt-optimization.title: The Programmatic Prompt Engineer

e2e-testing.name: QA Only
e2e-testing.description: Runs the scoped end-to-end test suite for a single feature or route without launching the full QA pipeline, for fast targeted regression checks.
e2e-testing.title: Gstack QA Only — Focused E2E Test Run

e2e-testing.name: QA
e2e-testing.description: Browser-driven web application testing that explores pages as a real user, documents bugs with annotated screenshots, fixes issues with atomic commits and re-verification, and produces structured reports with before/after evidence and health scores showing quality improvement.
e2e-testing.title: Gstack QA

write-report.name: Retro
write-report.description: Synthesises commit history, PR comments, and issue notes into a written sprint retrospective covering wins, misses, root causes, and action items.
write-report.title: Gstack Retro — Sprint Retrospective Report

write-report.name: README Generator
write-report.description: Analyzes a project's directory structure, dependency manifests, and configuration files to generate a professional README.md covering installation, usage, API reference, and contributing guidelines.
write-report.title: The Document Weaver

write-report.name: REST API README Generator
write-report.description: Scans a Java Spring project for controller annotations, extracts REST API endpoint definitions, and automatically generates structured API documentation in README format.
write-report.title: The Endpoint Scribe

knowledge-management.name: Setup GBrain
knowledge-management.description: Bootstraps the GBrain knowledge store for a new project: creates the index structure, ingests seed documents, and validates retrieval with a sample query.
knowledge-management.title: Gstack Setup GBrain — Knowledge Base Initialisation

knowledge-management.name: Sync GBrain
knowledge-management.description: Incrementally syncs new documents and updated pages into the GBrain index, deduplicates embeddings, and reports ingestion counts and any errors.
knowledge-management.title: Gstack Sync GBrain — Knowledge Base Sync

finishing-a-development-branch.name: Ship
finishing-a-development-branch.description: Automated end-to-end deployment workflow that merges the base branch, runs tests, reviews the diff, bumps the VERSION file, updates the CHANGELOG, commits, pushes to the remote, and creates a pull request in a single command.
finishing-a-development-branch.title: Gstack Ship

finishing-a-development-branch.name: Finishing a Development Branch
finishing-a-development-branch.description: Guides completion of development work by verifying tests first, then presenting structured options for merge, PR creation, or cleanup — never declaring done without a passing build.
finishing-a-development-branch.title: The Clean Landing

skill-authoring.name: Skillify
skill-authoring.description: Converts a freeform prompt, repo pattern, or workflow description into a complete, registry-ready named skill: writes the SKILL.md definition, populates frontmatter fields, and opens a PR for review.
skill-authoring.title: Gstack Skillify — Skill Authoring Pipeline

skill-authoring.name: Skill Builder
skill-authoring.description: Guides creation of new Ruflo skills through templating, testing, and publishing workflows.
skill-authoring.title: The Skill Forger

token-observability.name: CodeBurn
token-observability.description: Cost and token observability tool for tracking AI coding agent spending across models and projects.
token-observability.title: The Ledger of Light

api-call.name: HF CLI
api-call.description: Guides Hugging Face Hub CLI operations for downloading, uploading, managing repos, running Jobs, querying datasets, configuring Spaces, and handling Hub authentication.
api-call.title: The Hub Operator

data-analysis.name: Hugging Face Datasets
data-analysis.description: Explores Hugging Face datasets through the Dataset Viewer API, resolving configs and splits, previewing rows, paginating records, searching text, filtering rows, and retrieving parquet metadata.
data-analysis.title: The Dataset Cartographer

fine-tune.name: Hugging Face LLM Trainer
fine-tune.description: Runs LLM fine-tuning on Hugging Face Jobs using TRL or Unsloth, covering SFT, DPO, GRPO, reward modeling, dataset validation, hardware selection, Trackio monitoring, and Hub persistence.
fine-tune.title: The Cloud Fine-Tuner

literature-review.name: Hugging Face Papers
literature-review.description: Looks up Hugging Face paper pages and arXiv IDs, fetching markdown paper content and structured metadata such as authors, linked models, datasets, Spaces, GitHub repos, and project pages.
literature-review.title: The Paper Indexer

object-detection.name: Hugging Face Vision Trainer
object-detection.description: Trains and fine-tunes object detection, image classification, and SAM/SAM2 segmentation models on Hugging Face Jobs or locally with dataset validation, metrics, Trackio, and Hub persistence.
object-detection.title: The Vision Forge

semantic-cache.name: Semantic Cache
semantic-cache.description: High-performance semantic caching for LLM responses using embedding similarity.
semantic-cache.title: The Neural Memory

multimodal-reasoning.name: Transformers.js
multimodal-reasoning.description: Runs Hugging Face Transformers.js models directly in JavaScript and TypeScript across browser and server runtimes for NLP, vision, audio, and multimodal inference with WebGPU or WASM.
multimodal-reasoning.title: The Browser Modelwright

autonomous-research-agent.name: AutoResearch
autonomous-research-agent.description: Autonomous research agent that iteratively searches, reads, and synthesizes academic papers into structured summaries.
autonomous-research-agent.title: The Scholar's Compass

framework-upgrade.name: Upgrade Laravel v13
framework-upgrade.description: Guides an AI agent through upgrading a Laravel 12 application to Laravel 13 safely, covering breaking changes, dependency updates, config migrations, and post-upgrade test validation.
framework-upgrade.title: The Versionist's Trial

grill-me.name: Grill Me
grill-me.description: Conducts a relentless one-question-at-a-time interview about a plan or design, walking every branch of the decision tree with a recommended answer per question, resolving dependencies in order, and substituting codebase exploration wherever a question can be answered empirically.
grill-me.title: The Relentless Interviewer

grill-with-docs.name: Grill With Docs
grill-with-docs.description: The ultimate evolution of the grill pattern. Fuses relentless Socratic questioning with deep domain awareness, ensuring every decision is cross-referenced against a persistent ubiquitous language and documented via real-time ADR generation.
grill-with-docs.title: The Domain Sovereign

refactor-code.name: Improve Codebase Architecture
refactor-code.description: Identifies architectural deepening opportunities in a codebase — shallow modules with high interface-to-implementation ratios — using domain-glossary vocabulary and the deletion test, then grills the developer on the chosen candidate to design a deep-module replacement with better locality and testability.
refactor-code.title: The Depth Seeker

prd-generation.name: To PRD
prd-generation.description: Synthesises the current conversation context and codebase knowledge into a fully-structured PRD — problem statement, extensive numbered user stories, implementation decisions (modules, interfaces, schema), testing decisions, and out-of-scope items — then publishes it to the project issue tracker.
prd-generation.title: The PRD Synthesiser

issue-triage.name: Triage
issue-triage.description: Moves GitHub issues through a two-category (bug/enhancement) × five-state (needs-triage/needs-info/ready-for-agent/ready-for-human/wontfix) state machine. Reproduces bugs from issue steps, runs a domain-aware grilling session when needed, writes structured agent briefs, and appends AI-generated triage notes with the required disclaimer.
issue-triage.title: The State-Machine Triager

ubiquitous-language.name: Ubiquitous Language
ubiquitous-language.description: Extracts and formalises a project's domain terminology into a shared glossary, enforcing consistent naming across code and conversations to eliminate ambiguity.
ubiquitous-language.title: The Domain Linguist

ubiquitous-language.name: V3 DDD Architecture
ubiquitous-language.description: Applies Domain-Driven Design principles to the Ruflo v3 architecture including bounded contexts, aggregate roots, domain events, and ubiquitous language.
ubiquitous-language.title: The Domain Sculptor

code-explain.name: Zoom Out
code-explain.description: Signals the agent to ascend one layer of abstraction and produce a map of all relevant modules, callers, and domain-glossary terms in the unfamiliar code area, without explaining implementation details.
code-explain.title: The Abstraction Lift

design-generation.name: Open Design
design-generation.description: Local-first design engine for generating high-fidelity UI/UX prototypes and brand assets from structured intent.
design-generation.title: The Artisan's Forge

feed-monitoring.name: Feed Monitoring
feed-monitoring.description: Continuous monitoring of content feeds with intelligent discovery and state tracking.
feed-monitoring.title: The Signal Scout

brainstorming.name: Brainstorming
brainstorming.description: Explores user intent, requirements, and design before implementation. Turns ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.
brainstorming.title: The Collaborative Blueprint

dispatching-parallel-agents.name: Dispatching Parallel Agents
dispatching-parallel-agents.description: Delegates independent tasks to specialized agents with isolated context to work concurrently, dramatically reducing wall-clock time on multi-part problems.
dispatching-parallel-agents.title: The Parallel Dispatch

executing-plans.name: Executing Plans
executing-plans.description: Loads a written implementation plan, reviews it critically, executes all tasks sequentially, and reports when complete — without improvising beyond the plan's scope.
executing-plans.title: The Faithful Executor

receiving-code-review.name: Receiving Code Review
receiving-code-review.description: Processes code review feedback with technical rigor and verification before implementation — questioning unclear feedback and confirming fixes with tests rather than blindly applying suggestions.
receiving-code-review.title: The Rigorous Revision

requesting-code-review.name: Requesting Code Review
requesting-code-review.description: Dispatches a code reviewer subagent with isolated context to catch issues before they cascade — producing a structured review report that guides targeted fixes.
requesting-code-review.title: The Preemptive Review

subagent-driven-development.name: Subagent-Driven Development
subagent-driven-development.description: Executes plans by dispatching a fresh subagent per task with a two-stage review (spec compliance then code quality), keeping the orchestrator context clean throughout.
subagent-driven-development.title: The Swarm Architect

subagent-driven-development.name: Pair Programming
